query: IOC Policy Protection Female Category genetic screening LA28

{"query":"IOC Policy Protection Female Category genetic screening LA28","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1601,"verdict":null,"extra_info":{"type":"featured_snippet","text":"The IOC's new policy ensures that participation in the female category at IOC events, including the Olympic Games, is restricted to biological females. This is applicable for all sports – individual and team – from LA28 onwards and is not retroactive.May 22, 2026"},"results":[{"url":"https://www.olympics.com/ioc/athletes/medical-research/faq-ioc-policy-protection-female-category#:~:text=The%20IOC's%20new%20policy%20ensures,onwards%20and%20is%20not%20retroactive.","title":"Frequently Asked Questions about the IOC Policy on the Protection of the Female (Women’s) Category in Olympic Sport and Guiding Considerations for International Federations and Sports Governing Bodies","content":"The IOC’s new policy ensures that participation in the female category at IOC events, including the Olympic Games, is restricted to biological females.
This is applicable for all sports – individual and team – from LA28 onwards and is not retroactive.
The policy was adopted by the IOC Executive Board on 26 March 2026 and will be implemented for the first time at the LA28 Olympic Games.
Transgender athletes are not excluded from IOC events, including the Olympic Games.
All athletes participating in the boxing tournament of the Paris 2024 Olympic Games complied with the competition’s eligibility and entry regulations, as well as all applicable medical regulations at the time.
SRY gene screening does not require a developed medical system."},{"url":"https://www.olympics.com/ioc/news/international-olympic-committee-announces-new-policy-on-the-protection-of-the-female-women-s-category-in-olympic-sport","title":"International Olympic Committee announces new Policy on the Protection of the Female (Women’s) Category in Olympic Sport","content":"Following today’s approval of the policy by the IOC Executive Board, it will apply for the LA28 Olympic Games onwards, and is not retroactive.
Speaking about the policy, IOC President Kirsty Coventry said: “As a former athlete, I passionately believe in the rights of all Olympians to take part in fair competition.
The group also agreed that the most accurate and least intrusive method currently available to verify biological sex is screening for the SRY gene, a segment of DNA typically found on the Y chromosome that initiates male sex development in utero and indicates the presence of testes/testicles.
As a separate part of the working group, International Federation Chief Medical Officers representing individual and team sports were also included.
There is a press briefing with IOC President Kirsty Coventry today at 16:15 CET on this topic."},{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/sports/only-biological-females-determined-by-gene-screening-will-be-allowed-olympic-2026-03-26/","title":"Transgender athletes barred from female category events | Reuters","content":"LAUSANNE, Switzerland, March 26 (Reuters) - Only biological female athletes, whose gender has been determined by a one-time gene-screening test, will now be eligible to take part in female category events at the Olympic Games, the International Olympic Committee (IOC) said on Thursday."},{"url":"https://yipinstitute.org/policy/redefining-eligibility-in-olympic-sport","title":"Redefining Eligibility in Olympic Sport | YIP Institute Social Policy","content":"On March 26th, 2026, the International Olympic Committee (IOC) announced a new policy on the protection of the female (Women’s) category in the Olympic sports.
The policy mandates that eligibility for the female category is restricted to biological females only, verified through a one-time gene screening program.
The new policy will take into effect for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics, aligning with the U.S.
2026, apnews.com/article/ioc-olympic-transgender-female-eligibility-520cd9cee152a312767a667acf77dbc8."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/international-olympic-committee_the-ioc-has-announced-a-new-policy-on-the-activity-7442950047492657152-25O1","title":"IOC Introduces Policy for Female Category Protection in Olympic Sports | International Olympic Committee – IOC posted on the topic | LinkedIn","content":"Murdoch Children's Research Institute (MCRI) The Barr Body Test (1968–1992): Analyzed epithelial cells from a cheek smear to identify the inactivated second X chromosome (Barr body) typical of genetic females.
Starting with the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics, only biological females who pass a one-time SRY gene screening will be eligible to compete in female events.
"The International Olympic Committee (IOC) announced Thursday that it is adopting a new policy that will ensure only biological females compete in women's competitions."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"IOC Policy Protection Female Category genetic screening LA28","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7148904/2026/03/26/olympics-transgender-ban-women","title":"IOC says women’s Olympic events to be limited to ‘biological females’ - The Athletic","content":"# IOC says women’s Olympic events to be limited to ‘biological females’. The International Olympic Committee has announced compulsory gene testing that would bar transgender women from competing in women’s events. In a release on Thursday, the IOC said the new rule “protects fairness, safety and integrity in the female category” and does not apply to grassroots or recreational sports programmes. The testing, the IOC said, will also see some athletes with differences in sex development (DSD), as well as transgender women, prevented from competing. Athletes will have to undergo a one-time screening for the SRY gene, which is involved in male-typical sex development and is found on the Y chromosome. The gene testing, described as “evidence‑based and expert‑informed” by the IOC, will come into effect for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympic Games but will not be applied retroactively. The IOC said its decision was made after a working group it set up reached a consensus that “male sex provides a performance advantage in all sports and events that rely on strength, power and endurance.”.","score":0.7625837,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/sports/only-biological-females-determined-by-gene-screening-will-be-allowed-olympic-2026-03-26","title":"Transgender athletes barred from female category events","content":"## Browse World. *   New rules stop transgender athletes competing in female category events. LAUSANNE, Switzerland, March 26 (Reuters) - Only biological female athletes, whose gender has been determined by a one-time gene-screening test, will now be eligible to take part in female category events at the Olympic Games, the International Olympic Committee (IOC) said on Thursday. The new rules essentially stop transgender athletes from ​competing in female category events at the Olympics. All athletes wanting to qualify or take part in female category events from the LA2028 Olympics onwards will have to undergo an SRY gene test to determine their eligibility. \"With ⁠the rare exception of athletes with a diagnosis of Complete Androgen Insensitivity Syndrome (CAIS) or other rare differences/disorders in sex development (DSDs) who do not benefit from the anabolic and/or performance-enhancing effects of testosterone, no athlete with an SRY-positive screen is eligible for competition in the female category at an IOC event,\" the IOC said.","score":0.7197191,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/sport/olympics/articles/cdj7dgvlj0no","title":"Women's Olympic sports limited to biological females from 2028 - IOC - BBC Sport","content":"# Olympic women's sport limited to biological females. **The women's category of Olympic sports will be limited to biological females from 2028.**. The International Olympic Committee (IOC) says eligibility will be determined by a \"once-in-a-lifetime\" sex test, which would prevent transgender women and those with differences in sexual development (DSD) who have gone through male puberty from competing. \"So it is absolutely clear that it would not be fair for biological males to compete in the female category. The IOC said eligibility for the female category would be determined by a screening to detect the SRY gene - the sex-determining region Y gene - which is part of the Y chromosome and causes male characteristics to develop. A blanket ban on transgender athletes and DSD athletes from women's sport will be welcomed by many who have long felt that such a move is essential if fairness and safety in the female category is to be preserved.","score":0.6205532,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/thefemaleathleteproject/posts/the-international-olympic-committee-has-announced-a-new-policy-for-the-womens-ca/1421680729972434","title":"The International Olympic Committee has announced ...","content":"The International Olympic Committee has announced a new policy for the women's category at the Olympic Games, to be introduced from LA 2028.","score":0.575517,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.olympics.com/ioc/news/international-olympic-committee-announces-new-policy-on-the-protection-of-the-female-women-s-category-in-olympic-sport","title":"International Olympic Committee announces new Policy on ...","content":"Athletes who screen negative for the SRY gene permanently satisfy this policy's eligibility criteria for competition in the female category.","score":0.55275756,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.25,"request_id":"a830d04d-8ba0-4259-ac24-4eefcce3bac8"}
